diagnosis.final_diagnosis: right lower extremity ischemia with non healing ulceration (right sfa stent stenosis)
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient has a history of peripheral vascular disease (PVD) and multiple previous admissions for worsening right foot ulcers. Examination revealed erythema and significant edema around the ulceration, which does not probe to bone, suggesting a vascular etiology. The acute worsening of the ulceration following a treatment with IV vancomycin further supports ischemia related to stenosis of the right superficial femoral artery (SFA) stent.
